1-Clips that secure the front grill and font quarter panels have broken for no apparent reason. 2-Body parts can fall off during driving if not repaired 3-Inspected at dealership  4-No warnings - the front camera looked "cloudy" because it had fallen when the grill clips broke an

narrative.headline: 2020 Nissan Maxima: driver-assist and body complaints lead a small report pool
narrative.summary: Owners filed just 21 complaints with NHTSA, so the sample is small. Forward collision avoidance and structure are tied for the most reported areas at 19% each, followed by the brakes (14%), then the power train and electrical system (10% each). None of these reports involve a crash, fire, injury, or death.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: Tied for most reported: 4 reports (19%). No crashes, fires, or injuries are listed. Complaints describe driver-assist warnings firing on their own.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: Tied for most reported: 4 reports (19%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Owners mention body trim and clips coming loose.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 3 reports (14%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Reports describe the car braking on its own. Worth checking on a test drive.

issues.blurb: 2 reports (10%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. One owner describes jerking and a stall while driving.
